National — Education (current, from national.org.nz/education)

Teaching the Basics Brilliantly. National has put ambition and achievement back at the heart of the education system so Kiwi kids are set up for success — focused on the knowledge and skills they need in reading, writing and maths, and on raising achievement.

So far delivered:
- Mandated an hour of reading, writing and maths every day.
- Banned cell-phone use in schools so kids can concentrate better.
- Introduced a structured approach to teaching (structured literacy/numeracy) so all students master the basics.
- Announced a proposal to replace NCEA with a new national qualification built for tomorrow's workforce and higher learning.
- Released the full draft of New Zealand's new Year 1–10 curriculum.
- Delivered a large boost to learning support.

Approach: core knowledge and consistent standards, structured teaching methods, accountability for achievement, curriculum reform, reducing classroom distractions.
